english: put on, rgyan (rgyan) gay clothes, finery, rgyan bzáṅ-po btágs-pa (rgyan bzang po btags pa) beautifully attired Mil.; col. also without rgyan (rgyan), e.g. *túg-dad-can* (*thug dad can*) W. fond of dress and finery. — 2. in particular phrases: bkar-_o_dógs-pa (bkar 'dogs pa) v. bkar (bkar); mi-la skyon _o_dógs-pa (mi la skyon 'dogs pa) to charge a person with a fault, to upbraid; sgro _o_dógs-pa (sgro 'dogs pa) v. sgro (sgro); t'ugs-la _o_dógs-pa (thugs la 'dogs pa) to interest one's self in or for, to take care of; k'yod t'ugs-la mi _o_dógs-pa _o_di ci yin mi ṡes (khyod thugs la mi 'dogs pa 'di ci yin mi shes) why he does not interest himself in your behalf, I know not Mil.nt. 37, 6.; with reference to things: to have near at heart; t'ugs-la btágs-so (thugs la btags so) you have taken great care of me, a phrase frq. used, where we should say: I am much obliged to you! though Tibetans deny its implying acknowledgment and expression of thanks. — dám-la _o_dógs-pa (dam la 'dogs pa) v. dam (dam); paṇ _o_dógs-pa (pan 'dogs pa) v. páṇ-pa (pan pa); miṅ _o_dógs-pa (ming 'dogs pa) to give a name; drá-bai sgo-nas (dra ba'i sgo nas) according to likeness or analogy Mṅg.; k'yeui miṅ ci-skad gdags (khye'u'i ming ci skad gdags) how is the boy to be called? Dzl. miṅ mi-gdúṅ-ba ṡes (ming mi gdung ba shes) (or mi-gdúṅ-bar) (mi gdung bar) btágs-so (btags so) they named him... Mil., Dzl.; miṅ (ming) may also be wanting. — 3. Gram. to join, subjoin, affix, rar btags ga (rar btags ga) a g joined with r, i.e. rg (rg); ra-la ǰa (ra la ja) a ǰ joined with r, i.e. rǰ (rj); sa-la btags-pai ta-yig (sa la btags pa'i ta yig), st (st); ya-btags (ya btags), or shorter, yá-ta (ya ta), the ya which is written underneath, the subscribed ya, = ྱ; yá-ta btágs-pa yi-ge bdun (ya ta btags pa yi ge bdun), seven letters are joined with yá-ta(gs) (ya ta(gs)) Glr.; smád-_o_dogs gsum (smad 'dogs gsum) the three subscribed letters, ya, ra, and la Zam.; _o_dogs-can ('dogs can) 1. having a letter subscribed; 2. an open syllable with a vowel-sign, as go (go) གོ, de (de) དེ, mdo (mdo) མདོ, etc. (not da (da) ད or mda (mda) མདའ) Zam.; a-_o_dogs (a 'dogs) consonants with a (འ) subscribed, syllables with a long vowel. — 4. in philosophical writings: btágs-pa (btags pa) conditional, not absolute, Was. (228. 270), btags-med (btags med) nominal Was. (281).

pf. and imp. doṅ (dong) or _o_doṅ ('dong), to go, to proceed, so-sór (so sor) Dzl. to separate, to disperse; rgyál-poi t'ád-du (rgyal po'i thad du) (to go) to the king; p'yí-rol-tu _o_c'ág-_o_ciṅ (phyi rol tu 'chag 'ching) to take a walk Dzl.; dóṅ-ṅo (dong ngo) let us go Dzl.; lóg-la _o_doṅ-ṅo (log la 'dong ngo) let us turn back Glr.

I. vb. (W. more frq. tád-pa (thad pa)), to have a mind, to like, to be willing, zas bzáṅ-po mi _o_dód (zas bzang po mi 'dod) Dzl.; mi za _o_dod tsul byed (mi za 'dod tshul byed) he pretends not to like this food Lt.; sbyin(-par) _o_dód-pa _o_gyur (sbyin (-par) 'dod pa 'gyur) he gets inclined to give; mi _o_dód-par _o_gyúr-ba (mi 'dod par 'gyur ba) to feel no longer inclined; to wish, nyán(-par) (nyan (-par)) to listen; ci daṅ ci _o_dód-pa (ci dang ci 'dod pa) whatever you may wish Dzl.; rgyál-po _o_dód-pa (rgyal po 'dod pa) to wish to be a king Dzl.; as adj.: wished for, desirable, esp. with negatives, v. below; _o_dód-par byá-ba ('dod par bya ba) adj. agreeable, pleasing, obliging, flattering, Stg., Cs.; to desire to long for, k'yim _o_dód (khyim 'dod) I wish I were at home Dzl.; me daṅ nyí-ma (me dang nyi ma) (I am longing) for fire and for sunshine Med.; bú-mo ṅa mi _o_dód (bu mo nga mi 'dod) I do not wish for a girl; raṅ-_o_dód-żen-pa (rang 'dod zhen pa) self-love Glr.; (raṅ-) bzáṅ-_o_dód ((rang -) bzang 'dod) self-complacency, vanity, Glr.; to ask for, to demand, koṅ-jo _o_dód-pa-la slebs (kong jo 'dod pa la slebs) they came in order to ask for Koṅjo (Kong jo) (in marriage) Glr.; to strive for, to aspire after, saṅs-rgya-bar (sangs rgya bar) for holiness, for being like Buddha, for Buddhaship, Dzl.; to be willing, to intend; also ironically: ná-_o_dód-pa (na 'dod pa) one that wants to grow ill, that does not take any care of himself; to be ready, willing, bsnyen-bkúr byéd-par (bsnyen bkur byed par) to take charge of the waiting on (Buddha); _o_dód-par byéd-pa ('dod par byed pa) to make willing, disposed, to persuade to it Dzl.; to maintain, to assert; to suppose; to pronounce to be (cf. _o_tád-pa? ('thad pa?)) Mṅg., Tar. and elsewh. frq. — mi _o_dód-pa (mi 'dod pa) to be not willing, not liking; to detest, btsógs-pas kún-gyis mi _o_dód-na (btsogs pas kun gyis mi 'dod na) as she was detested by all on account of her sluttishness Dzl.; to be angry, indignant, żes mi _o_dód nas (zhes mi 'dod nas) thus exclaiming indignantly Dzl.; mi-_o_dód-pa (mi 'dod pa), and ma-_o_dód-pa (ma 'dod pa) adj. not wished for, disagreeable, adverse, mi-_o_dód-pai las (mi 'dod pa'i las) hard drudgery; mi-_o_dod(-lóg)-pai rluṅ (mi 'dod (-log) -pa'i rlung) adverse wind, frq.; *tsig mi - dód - pa zer - k'an* (*tshig mi dod pa zer mkhan*) W. one that slanders. II. sbst. 1. lust, desire in general; _o_dód-pa kun zád-de ('dod pa kun zad de) after all desires have ceased Dzl.; _o_dód-pa-rnams-la c'ágs-pa ('dod pa rnams la chags pa) to indulge one's desires or passions; in a special sense, carnal desire, lust, vo-
